meta_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity project template to Solve no version control (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Project Template for Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master version control and ship client projects faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template solves the 'no version control' crisis by embedding Git-ready structures directly into your scaffold. By pairing this with Claude Code, you automate commit history and branching, ensuring every client deliverable is tracked, versioned, and instantly recoverable—turning chaotic development into a professional, bulletproof pipeline.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, a client demands a rollback to last week’s build, and you realize you have zero version history. Your code is a fragile house of cards, and one wrong command could wipe out days of billable progress. The anxiety of 'no version control' is the silent killer of any freelance career.

But elite developers don't rely on luck. They use architectural templates to enforce discipline. By integrating the Antigravity project template, you move from manual file-swapping to a robust, automated workflow. This isn't just about saving code; it’s about professionalizing your output so you can ship faster, charge more, and sleep soundly.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Start by bootstrapping your project with the Antigravity template to establish a standardized directory structure that includes pre-configured Git hooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean project scaffolding for a web application that includes a .gitignore, README.md, and a structured src directory optimized for version control.

steps.title: Configure Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce commit standards, ensuring every change is logged with a meaningful message, preventing 'fix' or 'update' style commits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up husky or standard git hooks to enforce conventional commits for all future changes in this repository.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup git hooks for conventional commits'

steps.title: Establish the Primary Branching Strategy
steps.description: Define a simple main/feature workflow to isolate development from production, preventing accidental deployment of broken code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a README documentation file that outlines our branching strategy: main for production, develop for features.

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to generate project documentation based on your code structure, keeping your repo professional for client handovers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase and generate a comprehensive technical documentation file in the docs folder.

steps.title: The First Production Snapshot
steps.description: Verify your setup by creating a clean, tagged initial commit that serves as your project's baseline version.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add all files, commit with message 'Initial release: project scaffold', and tag as v1.0.0.
steps.claude_code_command: git add . && git commit -m 'Initial release' && git tag v1.0.0

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to track environment variables without exposing secrets to version control.
Use Claude Code to write your commit messages; it understands the context of your changes better than a rushed human.
Implement a 'pre-push' hook that runs your linter, ensuring only clean code ever reaches your remote repository.
Keep your commits atomic; never bundle a UI fix with a database migration in the same commit.
Use Git tags for every major client milestone; it makes rolling back to a specific invoice-ready state trivial.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: Now that your environment is secured, push your repository to a private GitHub or GitLab instance and configure a CI/CD pipeline to automate deployment whenever you merge to the main branch.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping like a pro. With Antigravity and Claude Code, you have the tools to scale your freelance business and eliminate technical debt forever. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
